ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly names Guru. Vision shows an enormous, corpulent elderly green-skinned figure seated on a throne-like seat in white and red robes — precisely the canonical depiction of Grand Elder Guru from Dragon Ball Z's Namek Saga. Canonical sources confirm he was massive, seated permanently due to extreme age, and wore layered robes. Card title 'Gift of Power' directly references his signature ability to unlock latent power in others.

trivia_bullets.text: Grand Elder Guru is the patriarch of all living Namekians at the time of Dragon Ball Z's Namek Saga — having single-handedly repopulated the species after a catastrophic climate event centuries prior by producing over 100 children. He is the creator of the Namekian Dragon Balls, meaning his death renders them permanently inert unless he transfers authority first. [WebSearch: Dragon Ball Wiki / Saiyan Warrior via search]

The 'Gift of Power' in the card title refers to Guru's ability to unlock dormant potential by placing his hand on a person's head. During the Namek Saga he unlocked the power of Krillin, Gohan, and Dende — raising Gohan's power level from approximately 1,500 to 14,000 and Krillin's to 13,000. The gift is a one-time bestowal per individual and is rooted in Guru's role as the supreme Namekian elder. [Kanzenshuu: Guru power-unlock discussion via WebSearch]

BT4-052 is a Common card — Guru sits at the lowest rarity tier in the BT4 Namekian cluster despite being narratively the patriarch of all Namekians. This inverts his in-universe importance and mirrors the support-role design: Guru in the anime never fights but enables others, which is typically the function of low-rarity support cards in the DBS card game. [dbs-cardgame.com: BT4 green strategy / DBZ Exchange]
